name: Temporal Equivalence Principle: A Blind-Prediction Residual Test in Multiply-Imaged Supernovae
description: SN Refsdal provides a rare blind-prediction test of potential-dependent temporal propagation: seven GR lens-modelling teams predicted the SX reappearance delay before the image was observed, while the later Kelly et al. (2023) measurement provides an independent comparator. This single-system probe reduces to a single-contrast measurement: 99.9% of the predicted proxy-model signal resides in the long-baseline S4--SX contrast, with effective dimensionality D_eff ≈ 2.0; the inner Einstein cross serves as a null region. The algebraic loop identity is insensitive to a uniform Mass Sheet Degeneracy, but the empirical blind-prediction residual remains lens-model-limited. The primary directional test is a Wilcoxon signed-rank test on six non-zero blind residuals, all positive (p = 0.016, ~2.2 sigma), matching the log-magnification proxy prediction. The supplementary all-eight-model Wilcoxon gives p = 0.0078 (2.4 sigma). Hierarchical Bayesian comparison remains inconclusive (BF ≈ 1.06 fixed-alpha, BF ≈ 0.49 free-alpha) at current lens-model uncertainties. The measured coupling alpha_lens = -0.055 +/- 0.044 is definitional, not independent; the probative content lies in sign consistency across independent modelling groups.
